title: The people neglect the LORD's house
summary_text: Haggai rebukes the returned community for living in paneled houses while the house of the LORD lies in ruins. He tells Zerubbabel and Joshua to consider their ways, and drought and shortage are explained as part of the covenant discipline. The chapter ends with the people stirred to obey and resume the work.
key_points: The people delay rebuilding the temple.
God interprets their hardship as discipline.
Haggai calls them to consider their ways.
The leaders and people obey the prophetic word.
evidence_phrases: they obeyed the voice of the LORD

title: The latter glory and Zerubbabel's signet ring
summary_text: Haggai encourages the builders not to be afraid because the LORD is with them and will shake the nations. He promises that the glory of the latter house will surpass the former and ends by setting Zerubbabel apart as a signet ring of chosen authority. The chapter strengthens the builders with future hope and present calling.
key_points: The builders are told to be strong and work.
God promises to shake heaven and earth.
The latter house will receive greater glory.
Zerubbabel is singled out as God's chosen signet ring.
evidence_phrases: the glory of this latter house
I will make you as a signet ring
